title: Book of Bhoots
description: Who whispers your name in the wind on this moonless night? Maybe nothing. Maybe something. Turn the pages of this book, and maybe you’ll hear it. And if you do, there’s no going back.

pages.text:
What wanders outside on this dark and moonless night?
What howls from the treetops, and whispers your name in the wind?

Maybe nothing.
Maybe something.
It depends on where you are.
It depends on what you believe.

Keep an eye on the ponds and rivers.
The Baak lives here.
The Baak has twisted arms and legs,
and its eyes and mouth are on its tummy.
BAAK

If you meet a Baak, don’t worry.
It is more interested in stealing
raw fish than in scaring you.
KHACH
KHACH
KHACH

Don’t look up at the skies at night.
The Jilaiya flies like a bird when it’s dark.
It sleeps when the sun shines.
JILAIYA

If you meet a Jilaiya, don’t let it hear
your name. It will suck your blood.
Shooop Shooop Shooop

Watch out for palm trees.
That’s where the Yakshi lives.
She floats above the ground and is always thirsty.
She takes her victims home and, the next day, there are bones under her tree.
YAKSHI

If you meet a Yakshi,
tell her not to come to tea.
Kuchal
Kuchal
Kuchal

Be careful when you walk near railway tracks.
The Skondhokata wanders here.
He has lost his head in a horrible way. Ouch!
He is looking for what he has lost.
SKONDHOKATA

If you meet a Skondhokata, he will ask you to search for his head too.
Chook
Chook
Chook

Beware the misty mountains.
The Rantas works her dark magic here.
She has long toenails and hair down to her knees, and her feet are turned backwards.
RANTAS

If you meet a Rantas, don’t go with her.
You will never come back.
Phish
Phish
Phish

Stay alert in the desert. The Ret Ro Pret hides here. It uses sand to change itself. One day a brown hill, the next day a wave in the sand.
RET RO PRET

If you travel to the desert, don’t wear browns and yellows. This sandy spirit will attack you.
Sssss
Sssss
Sssss

Keep away from moving lights in forests.
The Kolli Devva lurks here.
He walks through the night with a wooden torch, and dances and troubles travellers.
KOLLI DEVVA

If an invisible hand tries to light your lamp, scream and run.
Cham Cham Cham

So what wails outside your window on this black night?
You decide.

Maybe it’s a bird that sucks blood.
Maybe it’s a woman with feet turned backwards.
Maybe it’s the wind in an angry mood.

Skondhokata - West Bengal
Baak - Assam
Rantas - Kashmir
Ret Ro Pret - Rajasthan
Kolli Devva - Karnataka
Yakshi - Kerala
GHOST MAP OF INDIA
Jilaiya - Bihar
